rulings.Case Title: MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH ACCESS VS. DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Award Of Atty'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 31, 2013, Line 9, PETITIONER MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH ACCESS' motion For Award Of Attorney'S Fees. Granted in part. Petitioner is the prevailing party and conferred significant benefits on a large class of persons and thus is entitled to fees under Code of Civil Procedure 1021.5. However, because petitioner did not prevail on any claims for children under age 6 or on any state law or constitutional claims, there is a 50% reduction in hours for all work up to and including Judge Busch's order filed December 6, 2010 and no reduction thereafter. The hourly rates sought, which are based on rates charged by mid-size and large private firms, are excessive. Based on review of the papers filed in this case, my presiding over several hearings, and my experience as a trial judge who has handled dozens, if not hundreds, of motions seeking fees, the hourly rates for all timekeepers are reduced by 20% except the following whose hourly rates are reduced as stated: $500 for Ms. Quacinella and Mr. Keys and $550 for Mr. Rothschild. All fees up to and including the motion for fees are subject to a multiplier of 1.3 for the contingent risk. The parties are to meet and confer to calculate the total amount of fees based on the foregoing. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am or 2pm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE NORTON VS. HERTZ VEHICLES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 31, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT CAROLIN MAPLES' motion To Change Venue. Denied. The notice of motion fails to state the statutory basis for the motion. In any event, it is untimely in that Ms. Maples relies on Ms. Norton's responses from February 2012, and she has not provided any reason for her delay in seeking transfer earlier. Further, Ms. Maples has not made a sufficient showing that the convenience of witnesses or the ends of justice will be promoted by the change in venue. (CCP § 397(c).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JULIO NORBERTO ESBREZ VS. SF VERDI CLUB, INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To Propound Discovery Re: Financial Condition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 31, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF JULIO ESBREZ' Notice Of Motion For Leave To Propound Discovery Re: Financial Condition Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. Plaintiff did not provide sufficient evidence that that there is a substantial probability that the plaintiff will prevail on the punitive damages claim. Opposing Party shall prepare and bring a proposed Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is Granted and Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $2,500. Moving party shall prepare and bring a proposed Order.
